author: narahari⋅dāsa
verses:
mana re! kahanā gaura⋅kathā
gaurera nāma, amiyāra dhāma,
pirīti mūrati dātā

śayane gaura, svapane gaura,
gaura nayanera tārā
jīvane gaura, maraṇe gaura,
gaura galāra hāra

hiyāra mājhāre, gaurāṅge rākhiye,
virale basiyā ra’ba
manera sādhete, se rūpacā̐dere,
nayane nayane thoba

gaura vihane, na bā̐ci parāṇe,
gaura ka’rechi sāra
gaura baliyā, jāuka jīvana,
kichu nā cāhiba āra

gaura gamana, gaura gaṭhana,
gaura mukhera hā̐si
gaura⋅pirīti, gaura mūrati,
hīyāya rahala paśi

gaura dharama, gaura karama,
gaura vedera sāra
gaura caraṇe, parāṇa sā̐pinu,
gaura karibena pāra

gaura śabada, gaura sampada,
jā̐hāra hiyāya jāge
naraharidāsa, tā̐ra dāsera dāsa,
caraṇe śaraṇa māge
en_translation:
O mind! Speak the topics of Gaura. Gaura's Name is the abode of nectar, the giver of the form of love.

Gaura in sleep, Gaura in dreams, Gaura is the star of the eye. Gaura in life, Gaura in death, Gaura is the necklace of the neck.

Keeping Gaurāṅga within my heart, I will remain sitting in solitude. With the desire of the mind, I will place that Moon of Beauty eye to eye.

Without Gaura, I do not survive in life; I have made Gaura the essence. Let life go saying "Gaura"; I will not want anything else.

Gaura's gait, Gaura's form, the smile on Gaura's face; Gaura's love, Gaura's form—remained entered in my heart.

Gaura is Dharma, Gaura is Karma, Gaura is the essence of the Vedas. At Gaura's feet I have offered my life; Gaura will take me across.

He in whose heart the Sound of Gaura and the Wealth of Gaura awaken—Narahari Dāsa, the servant of His servant's servant, begs for shelter at the feet.
